source_pr.title: test: stabilize `TestStalenessAndHistoryRead`
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close #65621

Problem Summary: 

Fix flaky `TestStalenessAndHistoryRead` by eliminating reliance on local wall-clock time when asserting snapshot schema versions.

### What changed and how does it work?

- Use PD TSO-driven timestamps (via `@@tidb_current_ts` and last commit TS) and wait for a TSO strictly after the DDL commit before asserting snapshot schema versions, removing clock-skew and same-millisecond races.

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [ ] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [x] No need to test
  > - [x] Test-only change; not run locally (CI will cover).

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

root_cause_explanation: The staleness/history-read test derived timestamps from `time.Now()` plus fixed sleeps, which can drift relative to PD TSO and vary under CI load, producing inconsistent snapshot boundaries. This PR derives times from `@@tidb_current_ts`/last commit TS and waits for TSO time to advance, removing clock/timing flakiness.
fix_pattern: Avoid brittle fixed sleeps by tightening synchronization/wait conditions in the test.
